title: HMP Common Definitions
description: Shared type definitions for the Himeshaa Protocol v0.1.0 file schemas. These definitions are referenced by hmp-v1.json, memory-v1.json, contradiction-v1.json, and confirmation-v1.json via external $ref.

memory_context.description: Contextual metadata describing when and where a piece of knowledge applies. Used by memories, contradictions, and confirmations to enable similarity matching during retrieval.

stack.description: Technology stack identifiers for similarity matching. Use lowercase with version numbers where relevant (e.g., 'php-8.3', 'laravel-12', 'mysql-8'). This field drives the context similarity factor (S) in the confidence formula.

domain.description: Domain classification for similarity matching (e.g., 'web-application', 'cli-tool', 'machine-learning'). Used in canonical text construction for the context similarity factor (S).

files.description: Relevant file paths within the repository where this knowledge was observed or applies.

hmp_urn.description: A globally unique URN addressing an object in the Hive Mind. Format: urn:hmp:{node_uri}:{object_type}:{object_id}. The node_uri component is provider-agnostic (supports GitHub, GitLab, Gitea, Codeberg, self-hosted forges) and MUST be normalized to lowercase before storage and comparison. Character set follows RFC 8141 and RFC 3986 unreserved characters.

blob_sha.description: Git blob hash anchoring a cross-node reference to a specific content version. Accepts SHA-1 (40 hex characters) and SHA-256 (64 hex characters) to support both current Git repositories and the SHA-256 transition in Git 3.0+.

object_id.description: A unique identifier within the Node's namespace. MUST match the filename without the .json extension (e.g., id 'mem-001' corresponds to file 'mem-001.json').

utc_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 timestamp in UTC. MUST terminate with the Z suffix. Timezone offsets and fractional seconds are prohibited to ensure deterministic time decay computation across all implementations.

meta.description: Implementation-specific metadata extension point. Servers and agents MAY use this field to attach indexing hints, provenance data, or tooling metadata. Nesting SHOULD NOT exceed 2 levels of depth.
